original_abstract: Background: Medical Image segmentation is an important image processing step. Comparing images to evaluate the quality of segmentation is an essential part of measuring progress in this research area. Some of the challenges in evaluating medical segmentation are: metric selection, the use in the literature of multiple definitions for certain metrics, inefficiency of the metric calculation implementations leading to difficulties with large volumes, and lack of support for fuzzy segmentation by existing metrics.
humanized_abstract: Medical Image Segmentation is a crucial step in image processing. A key aspect of any research in this area is the comparison of images to assess the accuracy of the resulting segmentation. Several challenges have been reported in the literature such as: choosing the appropriate evaluation metric, using different definitions for the same metric and the fact that most of the current implementations are not efficient, which causes a bottleneck when dealing with large volumes of data. Also, current evaluation metrics are designed to deal with the classical, non-fuzzy case of image segmentation.

Also, current evaluation metrics are designed to deal with the classical, non-fuzzy case of image segmentation.
